column1: # Pride Travel Scout – New York City

column5: "USA: Extremely safe. Chelsea and Hell's Kitchen are iconic LGBTQ neighborhoods; rights strongly protected citywide."

column2: Four distinct seasons with hot humid summers and cold snowy winters.

column5: "Bitterly cold with freezing winds and frequent snow; heavy insulated winter layers required."

column6: often bringing the season's heaviest snow; waterproof winter footwear is essential."

column5: "Highly unpredictable transition from winter slush to cool sun; versatile mid-weight layers are best."
"Refreshingly mild but known for frequent spring showers; waterproof shells and light jackets recommended."
"Perfectly pleasant with low humidity and warm afternoons; ideal for light sweaters and t-shirts."
"Warm and increasingly humid with occasional evening thunderstorms; breathable cotton or linen fabrics work best."
"Peak summer heat and high humidity; stay hydrated and wear the lightest possible clothing."
"Oppressive humidity remains with very warm nights; moisture-wicking clothes are necessary for city walking."
"Warm golden days and crisp evenings; the most comfortable month for layering light fashion."
"Cool brisk air and bright autumn sun; medium-weight coats and boots are classic NYC staples."
"Sharp chilly winds signal winter's approach; warm wool coats and thermal accessories become necessary."
"Cold and festive but prone to biting wind-chill; full winter gear is needed for outdoors."
